payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Pocket money task as a compensated unit of work
Task assignment with associated monetary value
Completed task entity eligible for claiming
Child claims completed task for reward tracking
Track monetary rewards for completed tasks claimed by children
Support parent fulfillment of payment obligations to children

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task entity with associated monetary value that can be completed and claimed
payload.justification: SG291 explicitly defines task entities with monetary value attributes as a parent capability. | SG290 and SG292 establish completed tasks as distinct entities that can be claimed and linked to children.

payload.description: Child entity that can be linked to completed tasks
payload.justification: SG292 specifies child entities as participants in the relationship with completed tasks.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total aggregated by child
payload.justification: SG294 defines weekly aggregation of earned monetary totals as a visible data object.

payload.description: Historical task data retained across periods
payload.justification: SG297 mandates retention of historical task data as accessible entities.

payload.description: Child-task-earnings association tracking completed tasks for reward payment fulfillment
payload.justification: SG290 establishes the core claiming relationship; SG292 specifies the linkage mechanism. | SG295 explicitly defines the three-way relationship displayed in parent reporting.

payload.description: Weekly period for task availability and earnings aggregation
payload.justification: SG294 and SG296 both reference weekly cycles for aggregation and reset operations.

payload.description: Task completion event triggering availability for claiming
payload.justification: SG290 and SG293 imply task completion as a temporal event changing task state and visibility.

payload.description: Weekly boundary triggers task availability reset
payload.justification: SG296 explicitly defines the weekly boundary as a trigger for reset operations.

payload.description: Parent user who specifies tasks and views reports

payload.description: Track monetary rewards for completed tasks claimed by children and support parent fulfillment of payment obligations
payload.justification: SG290 establishes reward tracking as the core business objective of the system. | SG295 explicitly states payment fulfillment as the purpose of the reporting capability.

payload.description: Child visibility limited to available and completed tasks within temporal scope
payload.justification: SG293 defines visibility requirements as a normative constraint on child access.

payload.description: Historical task data must be preserved for temporal continuity
payload.justification: SG297 establishes data retention as a guiding principle for system design across periods.

payload.description: Task completion events must be associated with child entities
payload.justification: Signal describes an association rule governing how task completion must link to children, representing a logical business rule.

payload.description: Child users may access only their assigned task collections
payload.justification: Signal specifies data visibility requirements as access patterns, which translate to logical access control rules.

payload.description: Compute weekly aggregation of earned amounts
payload.justification: Signal describes temporal aggregation logic as a computational function performed weekly.

payload.description: Present aggregated earnings to child users

payload.description: Provide parent view of aggregated child earnings
payload.justification: Signal describes a reporting interface providing parents with aggregated data, representing a logical reporting function.

payload.description: Provide parent view of task completion data
payload.justification: Signal describes aggregated reporting of task completion to parents, representing a distinct reporting function.

payload.description: Reset task availability state
payload.justification: Signal describes state reset logic as a functional operation restoring task availability.

payload.description: Historical task records must be persisted and accessible
payload.justification: Signal mandates data retention as a requirement, representing a logical data persistence rule.

payload.description: Task completion event is associated with child entity
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a logical association rule linking task completion events to child entities, which is a clear relationship in the data model.

payload.description: Child user entity
payload.justification: Signal references child users as distinct data entities requiring access patterns, implying a logical entity in the data model.

payload.description: Task collection entity
payload.justification: Signal references task collections as data structures accessed by child users, representing a logical entity.

payload.description: Earned amount attribute of child entity
payload.justification: Signal describes earned amounts as data presented to children, representing an attribute computed and stored in the logical model.

payload.description: Child earnings data entity
payload.justification: Signal references child earnings as aggregated data accessible to parents, implying a logical entity in the data model.

payload.description: Task completion data entity
payload.justification: Signal references task completion data as reportable information, representing a logical entity.

payload.description: Task availability status attribute
payload.justification: Signal describes task availability as a state that is restored, representing an attribute of task entities.

payload.description: Historical task record entity
payload.justification: Signal explicitly mandates persistence of historical task records, indicating a distinct logical entity for historical data.

payload.description: Task completion event
payload.justification: Signal explicitly references task completion events as distinct temporal occurrences in the system.

payload.description: Weekly cycle for earnings computation and task availability reset
payload.justification: Signal describes weekly computation of earned amounts, representing a recurring temporal cycle. | Signal explicitly describes weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration, representing a recurring temporal pattern.

payload.description: Child user role
payload.justification: Signals reference child users as actors with specific access patterns and data presentations, representing a logical role.

payload.description: Parent user role
payload.justification: Signal references parents as actors receiving aggregated reporting data, representing a distinct logical role.

payload.description: Historical task records must be retained for future access
payload.justification: Signal mandates data retention as a requirement, representing a design constraint on data lifecycle management.

payload.description: Operating system version compatibility constraint for platform deployment
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete OS versions for deployment, which is a technical constraint at the Builder level governing deployment decisions.

payload.description: Child task visibility function displaying available and completed tasks within weekly cycles
payload.justification: SG541 defines operational visibility requirements for child actors, translating to a detailed function specification for displaying task states.

payload.description: Weekly earned amount total access event for child actors
payload.justification: SG542 establishes weekly operational schedule boundary as a timing specification when child actors access earned totals.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset cycle
payload.justification: SG544 specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets, defining a detailed recurring temporal cycle.

payload.description: Windows desktop platform deployment node
payload.justification: SG545 explicitly identifies Windows as a distinct named deployment platform requiring separate node specification.

payload.description: Parent user interface for task configuration and value assignment
payload.justification: SG540 identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary assignment, requiring detailed interface specification.

payload.description: Parent user interface for report access and distribution operations
payload.justification: SG543 defines parent operational role in accessing reports for pocket money distribution, requiring detailed interface specification.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
